nodes.label: NestJS Auth Module (JWT login, passport-jwt, RBAC with roles guard)
nodes.norm_label: nestjs auth module (jwt login, passport-jwt, rbac with roles guard)

nodes.label: Bilingual Naming Convention: PT-BR for business routes/features/URLs, English for NestJS modules and technical packages
nodes.rationale: NestJS modules align with packages/contracts and packages/database which are technical packages in English. Business URLs and features use Portuguese because the end user sees Portuguese in URLs, labels, and breadcrumbs.
nodes.norm_label: bilingual naming convention: pt-br for business routes/features/urls, english for nestjs modules and technical packages

nodes.label: Import Hierarchy Rules: app → features → components/ui → lib, with strict prohibitions on cross-feature imports and server/client boundaries
nodes.rationale: Prevents circular dependencies, enforces separation of concerns, ensures features are self-contained, and protects server-only code from leaking to the client.
nodes.norm_label: import hierarchy rules: app → features → components/ui → lib, with strict prohibitions on cross-feature imports and server/client boundaries

nodes.label: Feature Structure Pattern: features/<scope>/<dominio>/ with components/, types/, hooks/, schemas/, services/, actions/
nodes.rationale: Every feature must have at minimum components/, types/, and an index.ts barrel export. Pages in app/ only orchestrate — zero business logic in page.tsx.
nodes.norm_label: feature structure pattern: features/<scope>/<dominio>/ with components/, types/, hooks/, schemas/, services/, actions/

nodes.label: Mobile-First Design Paradigm: 320px baseline, cards not tables, thumb-friendly zones, touch targets >= 48px
nodes.rationale: Primary target audience is women accessing via smartphone (WhatsApp → landing page). All styles start mobile (no prefix), media queries add behavior upward (md:, lg:).
nodes.norm_label: mobile-first design paradigm: 320px baseline, cards not tables, thumb-friendly zones, touch targets >= 48px

nodes.label: Multi-Layer Validation Pipeline: Client Zod → NestJS ZodValidationPipe → Service business rules → Prisma schema
nodes.rationale: Client validates forms before sending. Next.js proxy does NOT validate — it only forwards. NestJS controller uses ZodValidationPipe with contracts/. Service adds business rules. Prisma validates schema.
nodes.norm_label: multi-layer validation pipeline: client zod → nestjs zodvalidationpipe → service business rules → prisma schema

nodes.label: Next.js API Proxy Pattern: app/api/[...route]/route.ts forwards requests to NestJS, never validates, never accesses DB
nodes.rationale: Next.js handles routing, SSR, and auth cookies on the front. NestJS runs on a separate port. In dev, Next.js proxies to NestJS. In production, Nginx/reverse proxy can bypass the Next.js proxy.
nodes.norm_label: next.js api proxy pattern: app/api/[...route]/route.ts forwards requests to nestjs, never validates, never accesses db

nodes.label: Single Source of Truth for Zod Schemas: packages/contracts/ is the sole source; NestJS DTOs re-export, never duplicate
nodes.rationale: Contracts define the exact API contract shape. NestJS must never redefine schemas — it re-exports from contracts. Feature-local schemas are only for UI-specific forms (wizards, confirm-password).
nodes.norm_label: single source of truth for zod schemas: packages/contracts/ is the sole source; nestjs dtos re-export, never duplicate

nodes.label: Mandatory UI States: Loading (skeleton), Empty with CTA, Error with retry, Success toast, Disabled visual
nodes.rationale: Every list component must implement all five states. Loading uses skeletons matching content structure, never 'Carregando...' text. Empty states include contextual CTAs.
nodes.norm_label: mandatory ui states: loading (skeleton), empty with cta, error with retry, success toast, disabled visual

nodes.label: Touch Target Specifications: buttons 48px min, card area 88px min, floating action 56px, thumb-friendly zones
nodes.source_location: Seção 9 — Touch Targets e Zonas de Toque
nodes.rationale: WCAG 2.2 Level AA compliance. Primary actions must be in the bottom half of the screen (thumb-friendly zone). Destructive actions must never be the easiest to reach.
nodes.norm_label: touch target specifications: buttons 48px min, card area 88px min, floating action 56px, thumb-friendly zones

nodes.label: Card vs Table Pattern: cards mandatory below 768px, table allowed at tablet+, full table at desktop
nodes.rationale: Tables are NEVER the primary experience for transactional data on mobile. Horizontal scroll for data is prohibited. Cards must be entirely tappable with min-height 88px.
nodes.norm_label: card vs table pattern: cards mandatory below 768px, table allowed at tablet+, full table at desktop

nodes.label: Filter Pattern by Breakpoint: bottom sheet on mobile, drawer on tablet, inline bar on desktop
nodes.rationale: Mobile uses Sheet with 85vh max from bottom. Tablet uses Drawer 400px from right. Desktop uses inline horizontal bar. Active filter count shown as badge on filter button.
nodes.norm_label: filter pattern by breakpoint: bottom sheet on mobile, drawer on tablet, inline bar on desktop

nodes.label: Pagination Pattern: Prev/Next large buttons on mobile, numbered pages on tablet+
nodes.rationale: Mobile shows 'Page X of Y' between Prev/Next buttons at 48px height. First page disables (never hides) Prev. Last page disables Next. Loading between pages shows skeleton.
nodes.norm_label: pagination pattern: prev/next large buttons on mobile, numbered pages on tablet+

nodes.label: Admin Navigation by Breakpoint: drawer from left on mobile, 64px icon sidebar on tablet, 240px fixed sidebar on desktop
nodes.rationale: Mobile: 56px fixed header with hamburger, drawer overlay from left with swipe-to-close. Tablet: 64px collapsed icons expandable to 240px. Desktop: 240px fixed sidebar with icons + labels + section groups.
nodes.norm_label: admin navigation by breakpoint: drawer from left on mobile, 64px icon sidebar on tablet, 240px fixed sidebar on desktop

nodes.label: Dashboard Layout by Breakpoint: 2 KPI columns mobile, 3 tablet, 4 desktop; graphs 200px mobile, 350px desktop
nodes.rationale: KPIs prioritized on mobile: revenue, active orders, pending orders, pending payments. Pie charts prohibited on mobile (illegible). Horizontal bar charts preferred. Tooltips must work with touch.
nodes.norm_label: dashboard layout by breakpoint: 2 kpi columns mobile, 3 tablet, 4 desktop; graphs 200px mobile, 350px desktop

nodes.label: Multi-Step Form Pattern: mandatory for 5+ fields on mobile, max 5 fields per step, sticky continue/back buttons
nodes.rationale: Labels always above inputs (never inline). All inputs min 48px height. Inline validation below fields. Submit button full-width sticky on mobile, auto-width inline on desktop.
nodes.norm_label: multi-step form pattern: mandatory for 5+ fields on mobile, max 5 fields per step, sticky continue/back buttons

nodes.label: Prohibited Anti-Patterns: feature cross-imports, app/ business logic, duplicated Zod schemas, hover-only actions, horizontal data scroll
nodes.rationale: 10 strict prohibitions enforced by code review: no feature-to-feature imports, no business logic in page.tsx, no domain knowledge in components/ui/, no duplicated schemas, no hover-only reveals, no horizontal data scroll.
nodes.norm_label: prohibited anti-patterns: feature cross-imports, app/ business logic, duplicated zod schemas, hover-only actions, horizontal data scroll

nodes.label: Architecture Governance: feature creation checklist, shared extraction rules, minimum feature requirements
nodes.rationale: New features follow 6-step process. Every feature must have components/, types/, index.ts. Shared code extraction rules: visual → components/ui/, logic → lib/, API types → packages/contracts/.
nodes.norm_label: architecture governance: feature creation checklist, shared extraction rules, minimum feature requirements

nodes.label: Definition of Done: 16-point checklist covering responsive breakpoints, UI states, accessibility, validation, and semantic HTML
nodes.rationale: Every feature must work at 6 breakpoints (320px-1440px), use cards below 768px, implement all UI states, pass WCAG AA contrast, support keyboard navigation, and use semantic HTML.
nodes.norm_label: definition of done: 16-point checklist covering responsive breakpoints, ui states, accessibility, validation, and semantic html

nodes.label: QA Checklist: 15-item mobile validation checklist tested on real device or DevTools at 320px and 375px
nodes.rationale: Validates no horizontal scroll, critical content visible without scroll, all buttons >= 48px, touch testing, skeleton loading, empty/error states, filter sheet, pagination, destructive confirmation dialog.
nodes.norm_label: qa checklist: 15-item mobile validation checklist tested on real device or devtools at 320px and 375px

nodes.label: PT-BR to EN Terminology Glossary: Pedido→Order, Prato→Dish, Cliente→Customer, Ciclo→WeeklyCycle, Assinatura→Subscription
nodes.rationale: UI uses Portuguese for user-facing text. Code uses English for technical symbols (NestJS modules, Prisma models, contracts). This glossary formalizes the mapping between the two.
nodes.norm_label: pt-br to en terminology glossary: pedido→order, prato→dish, cliente→customer, ciclo→weeklycycle, assinatura→subscription

nodes.label: Tailwind Breakpoints: sm=640px, md=768px, lg=1024px, xl=1280px, 2xl=1536px
nodes.rationale: All styles start mobile (no prefix). Media queries add behavior upward only (md:, lg:). Desktop-first CSS with mobile overrides via max-width is prohibited.
nodes.norm_label: tailwind breakpoints: sm=640px, md=768px, lg=1024px, xl=1280px, 2xl=1536px

nodes.label: Concrete Feature Example (orders/pedidos): barrels, types, schemas, services, hooks, components, and page orchestration
nodes.rationale: Demonstrates the complete feature anatomy with real code: index.ts barrel, types with PedidoFilter/PedidoListItem, schemas for form validation, services calling api-client, hooks managing state, and PedidoCard/PedidosLista components.
nodes.norm_label: concrete feature example (orders/pedidos): barrels, types, schemas, services, hooks, components, and page orchestration

nodes.label: Responsive Behavior Rules v1: card vs table breakpoints, filter strategies, pagination patterns, form layout per device
nodes.rationale: Mobile: cards only, bottom sheet filters, Prev/Next pagination. Tablet: table with essential columns, drawer filters. Desktop: full table, inline filters, numbered pagination. Same core principles as v2 but less exhaustively specified.
nodes.norm_label: responsive behavior rules v1: card vs table breakpoints, filter strategies, pagination patterns, form layout per device

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Rules: query/explain/path tools for codebase navigation, update after code changes
nodes.norm_label: graphify knowledge graph rules: query/explain/path tools for codebase navigation, update after code changes

hyperedges.label: Mobile-First Design System: patterns that collectively implement the mobile-first paradigm across all UI surfaces
Architecture Governance Framework: rules and checklists ensuring architectural consistency
